skill.name: project-research
skill.description: Guides structured project research with scoped questions, approved sources, citation tracking, and synthesis steps.
skill.summary: Coordinate project research with clear scope, source selection, citations, and synthesis.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged three external command patterns and two weak cryptography patterns, but all referenced lines are markdown content rather than executable code. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or command execution evidence was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but lines 78 through 83 are a fenced markdown example for documenting a project pattern. The text is not executable code and does not ask the model to run a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a markdown code fence containing placeholders for a documentation pattern. There is no shell, Ruby, subprocess API, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Boundary Treated as Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at lines 83 through 88, but the content is a closing markdown fence followed by an integration heading and plain list text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text closes a markdown block and lists integration targets. It contains no executable command syntax or instruction to execute external programs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Paths Treated as Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at lines 88 through 90, but the text only names project paths and related integration locations in markdown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown bullets with inline code formatting for paths. They do not invoke external commands or construct a command string.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Treated as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3, but the line is frontmatter description text about research source management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains natural language metadata and no cryptographic function, hash name, library call, or security-sensitive operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Table Header Treated as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at line 16, but the line is a markdown table header for skill variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 16 contains column labels in a markdown table. It does not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Project research often becomes inconsistent when scope, sources, and citations are handled ad hoc. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable workflow for scoped research and attributed synthesis.
actual_capabilities: Defines research scope with primary questions, sub-questions, boundaries, and source types.
Guides source selection across web search, documentation, and academic sources.
Supports citation recording and confidence notes for each source.
Provides synthesis steps for findings, disagreements, and confidence assessment.
Includes a template area for project-specific research patterns and approved sources.
limitations: It is a workflow template and does not provide built-in research automation.
Project-specific sources and patterns must be added before high-trust use.
It depends on available tools for web search, documentation access, or subagents.
It does not verify facts without access to external sources.

use_cases.title: Scope Technical Research
use_cases.description: Define the research question, boundaries, and required source types before comparing implementation options.

use_cases.title: Prepare Evidence-Based Briefs
use_cases.description: Collect citations, note confidence levels, and synthesize findings into a concise project brief.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Research Agents
use_cases.description: Split a broad question into sub-questions and assign source-specific work to available research subagents.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Research Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project-research workflow to define scope for this question: [question]. Include sub-questions, boundaries, and source types.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Source Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [topic] using documentation and recent web sources. Track citations, confidence, agreement, and disagreements before summarizing.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Project Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a project-specific research pattern for [domain]. Include trigger conditions, actions, approved sources, and expected output format.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Parallel Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Break [research question] into subagent tasks. Define each sub-question, source type, expected evidence, and final synthesis structure.

output_examples.input: Research whether a project should adopt a new search library.
output_examples.output: A scoped brief with the main question, source plan, documentation citations, tradeoffs, confidence notes, and a final recommendation.

output_examples.input: Summarize competing claims from documentation and recent articles.
output_examples.output: A synthesis that separates confirmed facts, disagreements, source quality, and open questions that need follow-up.

output_examples.input: Create a repeatable research pattern for dependency evaluation.
output_examples.output: A reusable pattern with trigger conditions, required evidence, source priorities, and a standard decision summary.

best_practices: Customize approved sources before using the workflow for important decisions.
Record confidence levels and citations while gathering evidence.
Separate verified findings from assumptions and unresolved questions.
anti_patterns: Using the template without replacing project-specific placeholders.
Making claims without citations or source confidence notes.

faq.question: Does this skill perform research automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow that guides research when the assistant has access to suitable tools and sources.

faq.question: Can it use web search?
faq.answer: Yes, if the host assistant has web search available. The skill itself does not add network access.

faq.question: Does it require subagents?
faq.answer: No. Subagents are optional and useful only when the environment supports coordinated research agents.

faq.question: How should teams customize it?
faq.answer: Teams should add approved sources, domain vocabulary, output formats, and quality criteria for their project.

faq.question: Can it support academic research?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes academic sources as an approved source type for foundational concepts.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use approved internal sources and avoid exposing confidential details to external tools unless your policy allows it.
